params.diff:
diff --git a/CHANGELOG.md b/CHANGELOG.md
--- a/CHANGELOG.md
+++ b/CHANGELOG.md
@@ -1,3 +1,53 @@
 # Changelog
 
+## v1.0.0-beta.3 - 2026-05-18
+
+- Added the LiveWorkRail above the composer for live edited-files
+  tracking, with per-file inline diff expansion and a sticky file
+  header so scrolling through long diffs keeps the collapse control
+  reachable.
+- Cleaned up desktop build noise by silencing Swift recorder warnings,
+  removing redundant main-process dynamic imports, and allowing the
+  ffmpeg installer postinstall script needed for release packaging.
+- Updated the release workflow artifact actions to versions that run
+  on the Node.js 24 action runtime.
+- Aligned the rail-level chevron with the [hidden] attribute so
+  collapsing the rail actually hides its body in real CSS engines
+  (previously a flex display rule was silently overriding it).
+- Merged the section-level heading text into the rail title so the
+  summary count + line stats live on a single chevron-bearing line.
+- Tightened the rail's width to match the composer's reading column
+  via --chat-column-max so the edges line up visually across the
+  primary chat surface.
+- Dropped redundant aria-labels on inner section elements that
+  duplicated the rail-level complementary landmark name.
+- Switched idle file-toggle background to --bg-panel so rows sit
+  visually inside the rail card instead of merging flush with its
+  --bg-panel-elevated chrome.
+- Gated the editedFilesEntry on having at least one diff-bearing
+  detail so a malformed entry can't claim a title without
+  rendering anything below it.
+- Routed the persisted activity diff through deferLiveTranscriptEntry
+  on turn/completed so the transcript receives a single canonical
+  copy instead of double-rendering against the rail's pending
+  snapshot.
+- Locked in the merged-title shape with new vitest cases covering
+  the comma-join logic, the empty-fileDiff guard, and the rail's
+  pinned-to-last-turn snapshot lifecycle.
+- Added new Playwright e2e cases that exercise the chevron toggle,
+  per-file expansion, and title rendering inside Electron's real
+  CSS engine so display:flex vs [hidden] regressions can't sneak in
+  the way they did the first time.
+- Replaced the in-transcript Edited N files rows with the rail's
+  cumulative summary plus per-file expansion so the user no longer
+  sees the same file in two competing surfaces during an active
+  turn.
+- Improved sticky positioning so the file-toggle header pins to
+  the very top of the rail's scroll viewport with no leftover
+  padding gap.
+
 ## v1.0.0-beta.2 - 2026-05-18
 
 - Fixed universal macOS release packaging so the prebuilt window-list helper
